data.title: Asana Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.05, revenue of $183.9M beats by $3.24M
data.description:
• Asana press release (NYSE:ASAN): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $183.9M (+10.5% Y/Y) beats by $3.24M.

  • For fiscal 2025, Asana expects:

  • Revenues of $723.0 million to $724.0 million, representing year over year growth of 11%.

  • Non-GAAP operating loss of $46.0 million to $45.0 million, with 6% operating loss margin.

  • Non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.15 to $0.14, assuming basic and diluted weighted average shares outstanding of approximately 229 million.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, Asana expects:

  • Revenues of $187.5 million to $188.5 million, representing year over year growth of 10%.
  • Non-GAAP operating loss of $6.5 million to $5.5 million, with 3% operating loss margin.
  • Non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.02 to $0.01, assuming basic and diluted weighted average shares outstanding of approximately 229 million.

data.title: Netflix continues gains for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) shares continued gains for seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 0.8% higher at $917.87 on Thursday.

The California-based streaming giant gained about 4% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock has gained 89% so far this year, outperforming the nearly 28% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

NFLX is up 21% over the past one month. The stock closed nearly 1% higher on Wednesday at $911.06.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NFLX has a Hold rating with a score of 3.48 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D- in valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 28 analysts gave NFLX a Buy and above. 14 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and four recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

The company, in October, impressed investors with its third quarter earnings report as it topped expectations for profits and user growth.

“Netflix's superior content, recommendations, and user interface give it a competitive edge in the streaming space, attracting and retaining subscribers,” noted a recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Tangerine Tan Capital.

However, another Seeking Alpha analyst Kevin George said Netflix's subscriber growth may be plateauing and despite record-high net margins from restructuring, cost of revenue has stabilized, indicating potential margin pressures ahead.

data.title: Xpeng's bet on EREVs might not pay off - analyst
data.description:
Shares of Xpeng (NYSE:XPEV) have dropped by more than 2% in late trade on Thursday after the stock was dealt a downgrade at UBS on questions whether the newly launched MONA M03 and P7 monthly delivery goals are sustainable.

UBS analyst Paul Gong lowered his rating on the Chinese automaker to Sell from Neutral as a warning to investors of Xpeng’s (XPEV) downside risks that are not yet priced in.

“There’s no easy money in the [extended range electric vehicle market],” Gong writes, questioning the consensus that extended range electric vehicles (“EREV”) vehicles will outsell battery-electric vehicles (“BEV”).

EREVs is a hybrid vehicle that combines an electric motor with a small ICE to extend the vehicle's driving range.

Besides the potential for subdued demand, the visibility on Xpeng’s (XPEV) EREV pipeline remains unclear. The view that EREVs will outsell BEVs with the same remaining specs is not a general rule, Gong says, adding that whether EREVs can help Xpeng (XPEV) scale and narrow operating losses still hinges on target consumer understanding of EREVs, company strategy execution, and EREV brand recognition.

“While we share the market’s excitement for XPeng’s new EV momentum and fast cost optimization, we believe the upside is more than priced in,” Gong says in his research note.

Xpeng (XPEV) is heading into the close in the red for the first time in six days.

data.title: nCino stock slides 12% after soft Q4 guidance, Piper downgrade
data.description:
nCino (NASDAQ:NCNO) stock dropped 12% in late Thursday trading after the company issued weak guidance for Q4 revenue late Wednesday. Piper Sandler downgraded the stock to Neutral from Overweight, citing revenue headwinds for the full year.

The company's Q4 revenue guidance of $139.5M-$141.5M fell short of the consensus estimate of $143.8M. However, nCino's (NASDAQ:NCNO) Q3 non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 and revenue of $138.8M both beat the Wall Street consensus estimates.

Piper Sandler analyst Brent A. Bracelin said subscription revenue contributed to the $1.8M revenue beat, but "unexpected churn tied to industry consolidation within the mortgage segment (15% of sales) overshadowed a $6M q/q increase in top-line revenue (highest dollar change on a sequential basis in 2+ years)."

Q4 mortgage growth could slip to under 5%, the analyst added, tempering Piper's expectations of a recovery in growth next year, at least until mortgage volumes begin to improve materially.

The Neutral rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Bitcoin retreats from $100K, bringing down other crypto, related stocks
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) retrenched some on Thursday afternoon after holding above the $100K mark for less than 24 hours.The token rose as high as $104K, a rally of almost 50% since Trump's presidential victory, that was given a last push by Trump's pick of pro-crypto Paul Atkins to head the Securities and Exchange Commission.

At 3:15 PM ET, the oldest of digital tokens traded at ~$98,5K, according to CryptoCompare. Many other crypto assets also pulled back. Ethereum (ETH-USD) slipped 0.5% to $3.83K, solana (SOL-USD) dipped 0.3%, cardano (ADA-USD) - 0.5%, and XRP (XRP-USD) -2.2%.

Bitcoin-linked stocks followed suit. MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) slid 5.1%, Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) -2.5%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) dipped 3.7%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -2.5%, Bit Digital (BTBT) -7.1%.

data.title: HSBC's private-banking CEO leaving at month's end
TotalEnergies upgraded at RBC with 'quality on sale' after stock price lags peers
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) +1.4% in Thursday's trading as RBC Capital upgrades to Outperform from Sector Perform with an €80 price target, seeing the stock's recent selloff as providing an opportunity for long term investors to build a position ahead of material growth in the coming years.

TotalEnergies (TTE) has materially underperformed the sector recently, which RBC's Biraj Borkhataria attributes to uncertainty in French politics as well as company specific issues related to investments in Adani, which has raised questions around the company's due diligence.

The company also has been weighed by some soft results and persistent downgrades to earnings potential for liquefied natural gas, the analyst says.

Borkataria notes TotalEnergies (TTE) does not have material exposure to French corporate tax given most of its profits are derived from outside the country, while a buyback tax also likely would be immaterial in the context of the wider group.

While the risk premium around French politics could linger for some time, but Borkhataria believes the actual monetary risk is more than reflected in TotalEnergies' (TTE) lagging performance compared to peers.

data.title: Lilly's Zepbound will first be given in the UK to those with 'highest clinical need'
data.description:
Given the strong demand expected for Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) weight loss treatment Zepbound (tirzepatide) in the UK, the country's National Institute for Health and Care Excellence ("NICE") said the drug will first be offered to people with the "highest clinical need."

Zepbound has been recommended by NICE for those with a body mass index of greater than 35 and at least one illness related to being overweight. The organization, responsible for ensuring evidence-based best practices and value for health care, said that about 3.4M people in the country meet these criteria.

"The roll out of tirzepatide has to be carefully managed to ensure healthcare professionals can continue to meet the full range of health needs of all their patients," NICE said.

Final guidance is expected on Dec. 23, barring any appeals. Assuming there aren't, the first patients can receive Zepbound after 90 days if they are already receiving weight management services, or 180 days if they have the highest clinical priority.

NICE added that about 220,000 people are expected to get Zepbound during the three-year rollout period. "During this time evidence will be generated to understand the most effective forms of care, and this will inform a NICE re-evaluation of the remaining roll out period to the wider group of eligible patients."

data.title: Bantec's Howco Distributing and Bantec Environmental win contracts
data.description:
• Bantec (OTCPK:BANT) has won contracts through its subsidiaries Howco Distributing and Bantec Environmental.
  • "Howco Distributing had sales of 1.163M for the past three months with one sizable contract of $0.46M." said Michael Bannon, Bantec's Chairman and CEO.
  • Bantec Environmental won its first fire cleanup contract; Bantec Environmental is now a licensed asbestos removal contractor in Connecticut.
